---
title: "Contributing"
---

# Contributing

Contributions are welcome. This guide covers the setup, conventions, and workflow for working on Mayon.

## Prerequisites

- **Node.js 22** — set via `.nvmrc`; use `nvm install` and `nvm use`.
- **pnpm 10** — set in `packageManager`; install with `corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@10 --activate`.
- **Docker** — required to run Postgres and the Mayon server.

## Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/bendlikeabamboo/mayon.git
cd mayon
pnpm install
```

## Code style

Mayon uses ESLint (flat config) and Prettier. Do not add comments to code unless specifically asked.

```bash
pnpm lint       # check for issues
pnpm format     # auto-fix formatting
```

CI runs both. Pushing code that fails lint or format checks will block the merge.

## Type checking

Run the type checker before pushing:

```bash
pnpm check
```

This runs `svelte-check` across the entire codebase.

## Branch and commit conventions

- Branch names: `feature/short-description`, `fix/short-description`, `chore/short-description`.
- Commit messages: imperative mood, short subject line. Examples: `add provider template for Ollama`, `fix branch context assembly for nested trees`.
- Keep commits small and atomic. Each commit should represent one logical change.

## Pull request flow

1. Create a feature branch from `main`.
2. Implement the change and ensure `pnpm check`, `pnpm lint`, and `pnpm test` all pass.
3. If you added or changed the database schema, run `pnpm db:generate` and commit the results.
4. Open a pull request against `main`.
5. Address review feedback and update the branch.

## Testing

Tests run against a pglite (Postgres-compatible) test driver.

```bash
pnpm test         # run once
pnpm test:watch   # watch mode
```

Write tests for new repositories, provider adapters, and context assembly logic. UI component tests are added as the surface grows.

## Code of Conduct

See [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](https://github.com/bendlikeabamboo/mayon/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) for community guidelines.
